steps.title: Count the data set and identify positive numbers
steps.content:
The data set is: $-11$, $-9$, and $26$.
There are 3 total numbers to choose from.
Checking which are positive: $-11$ is negative, $-9$ is negative, and $26$ is positive. So there is 1 positive number.

steps.title: Calculate the probability
steps.content: Probability equals favorable outcomes divided by total outcomes: $$P(\text{positive}) = \frac{1}{3}$$ That matches choice B — B is correct.
